evidence: Core fix in service.rs:219-224 removes the two lines that overwrote `envelope.metadata.backend_used` and `envelope.metadata.model_used` with `request.resolved_target` values, allowing adapter-reported metadata to survive to downstream consumers.

completion.rs: `invoke_completer` return type changed from `Value` to `InvocationEnvelope`; `RecordProducer::Agent` at line 138-141 now uses `envelope.metadata.backend_used.family` and `envelope.metadata.model_used.model_id` instead of `completer_target.backend.family` / `completer_target.model.model_id`.

prompt_review.rs: `invoke_panel_member` return type changed from `Value` to `InvocationEnvelope`; both refiner (line 111-114) and validator (line 170-173) `RecordProducer::Agent` constructions now source from envelope metadata.

final_review.rs: All five `RecordProducer::Agent` constructions (reviewer proposal at 239, planner vote at 351, reviewer vote at 443, arbiter at 559, plus error-path variants) now use envelope metadata instead of configured target.
engine.rs:4648-4650 was already using `envelope.metadata` â€” no change needed; this was the only correct call site before the fix.
fs.rs:1105-1106 serializes `ArtifactRecord` via `serde_json::to_string_pretty` without transformation, so the corrected producer metadata flows through to `history/artifacts/*.json` files.

Two test additions provide coverage: service.rs unit test `invoke_preserves_adapter_reported_backend_and_model_metadata` with DivergentMetadataAdapter asserts Codex/actual-model survive invocation; final_review.rs e2e test `persisted_artifacts_use_envelope_metadata_not_resolved_target` runs the full panel and asserts all persisted artifact JSON files carry envelope metadata (codex/actual-model-from-envelope), not configured targets (claude/configured-*).

Remaining uses of configured target in `ReviewerProposalRecord` fields (final_review.rs:272-273) and `ResolvedTargetRecord` (engine.rs:6669-6670) are for different purposes (membership tracking and resolution snapshots respectively) and are correctly unchanged.
findings_or_gaps: Implementation description inaccurately claimed changes were 'isolated to src/contexts/agent_execution/service.rs' â€” actual changes span 4 source files (service.rs, completion.rs, prompt_review.rs, final_review.rs). The additional changes are correct and necessary; the downstream call sites were independently constructing RecordProducer from configured targets rather than envelope metadata.

Branch includes unrelated changes in process_backend.rs (debug eprintln! logging for parse failures, --debug-file CLI arg addition, signal handling refactor into send_signal helper) and the result symlink. These are outside the scope of the stated fix.

Rust toolchain (cargo, rustc, rustfmt) is unavailable in the review environment, so the new tests and overall compilation could not be verified by execution â€” review is limited to static inspection.
follow_up_or_amendments: Run `cargo test invoke_preserves_adapter_reported_backend_and_model_metadata persisted_artifacts_use_envelope_metadata_not_resolved_target` and `cargo check` in a Rust-toolchain-equipped environment to validate compilation and test correctness.
Consider separating the unrelated process_backend.rs changes (debug logging, signal refactoring) into a distinct commit or branch to keep the artifact-metadata fix cleanly scoped.
